title: statdown DESIGN
text: Scope: statdown lets bloggers add R content existing static site without replacing site generator. knits .Rmd CommonMark .md uses depkit manage htmlwidget CSS/JS dependencies build time. output plain Markdown SSG (Hugo, Jekyll, Eleventy, etc.) consumes directly — Pandoc, second HTML engine.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Keep SSG charge site builder (Hugo, Jekyll, Eleventy, Markdown engine) HTML engine pipeline. statdown produces .md asset files — never calls Pandoc never produces HTML documents. Shortcodes, templates, front matter remain SSG’s business. Minimal surface area One public function: statdown_render(). Everything else internal. First-class htmlwidget support via depkit Widgets render inline HTML fragments .md output. CSS/JS dependencies deduplicated, copied output directory, injected <link> / <script> tags depkit, CDN delivery SRI integrity default. Build-time responsibility boundaries statdown handles build-time knitting dependency collection. downstream engine handles Markdown--HTML conversion render time.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Replace blogdown, rmarkdown, Quarto. (Quarto right tool R center site rather occasional guest.) Full site orchestration (finding posts, running Hugo, managing archetypes). Supporting Pandoc features (citations, cross-refs, multi-format output).

title: statdown DESIGN
text: statdown knitr wrapper two responsibilities: Knit .Rmd .md predictable chunk defaults suitable static-site Markdown engines. Capture materialize widget dependencies using depkit build step, emitting <link> <script> tags directly .md output.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Validates input file required packages. Creates depkit DependencyManager render context, passing cdn enable CDN delivery SRI integrity. Sets knitr options chunk defaults (including custom render function routes htmlwidgets statdown’s dep-tracking path). Runs knitr::knit(). knitting, injects CSS tags top JS tags bottom .md widgets encountered. Cleans render context via .exit().
code: statdown_render(input, output_root = NULL, url_root = NULL, cdn = TRUE, quiet = FALSE)

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Render context (context.R) — module-level environment (.context) holding active DependencyManager. Provides init_context(), get_dm(), set_dm(), clear_context(). knitr print method (custom-render.R) — knit_print.htmlwidget() extracts widget dependencies, registers depkit, returns widget HTML knitr::asis_output(). knitr configuration (render.R) — set_suggested_options() sets device, DPI, figure dimensions, message/warning defaults, custom render chunk option (statdown_knit_render) ensures htmlwidget output routed statdown’s dep-tracking print method. Environment validation (validate-environment.R) — validate_environment() checks knitr, depkit, htmltools, svglite installed. S3 registration (zzz.R) — .onLoad registers knit_print.htmlwidget via registerS3method() knitr’s namespace. fallback; primary dispatch path custom render chunk option (see “Widget dispatch” ).

title: statdown DESIGN
code: flowchart TD   A["input.Rmd"] --> B["statdown_render()"]   B --> C["validate + init DependencyManager"]   C --> D["set knitr options"]   D --> E["knitr::knit()"]   E --> F["output.md (raw)"]   E --> G["widget chunks trigger knit_print.htmlwidget"]   G --> H["deps registered with depkit via insert()"]   H --> I["updated DM stored in .context"]   F --> J{"any deps registered?"}   J -- yes --> K["emit_css(dm) prepended to .md"]   J -- yes --> L["emit_js(dm) appended to .md"]   K --> M["output.md (final)"]   L --> M   J -- no --> M

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Widget dependencies injected raw HTML directly .md output: <link> tags (CSS) prepended Markdown content. <script> tags (JS) appended Markdown content. approach chosen shortcode/manifest mechanism simpler, renderer-agnostic, requires Hugo-specific templates configuration. widgets encountered knitting, .md output left unchanged — empty tags, markers.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: statdown_render() creates short-lived render context stored module-level environment (.context): context holds single field — active DependencyManager — exposes four internal functions: depkit’s insert() returns new InsertUpdate containing updated DependencyManager, callers must call set_dm(depkit::dm(update)) insertion persist change.
code: .context <- new.env(parent = emptyenv()) .context$dm <- NULL

title: statdown DESIGN
text: knitr encounters htmlwidget chunk, dispatches knit_print.htmlwidget(), : Extracts htmlDependency objects via htmltools::findDependencies(x). Calls depkit::insert(dm, deps) register . Stores updated DM back via set_dm(depkit::dm(update)). Renders widget HTML via htmltools::renderTags(x). Returns HTML string wrapped knitr::asis_output() knitr passes without escaping.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: htmlwidgets ships knit_print.htmlwidget method re-registers knitr’s S3 method table via s3_register() inside .onLoad. library(DT) (widget package) triggers .onLoad, htmlwidgets method overrides earlier S3 registration — including . statdown solves custom render chunk option set set_suggested_options(): statdown_knit_render(x, options, ...) checks inherits(x, "htmlwidget") calls statdown’s knit_print.htmlwidget directly, bypassing S3 dispatch entirely. objects falls knitr::knit_print(). S3 registration .onLoad (asNamespace("knitr")) retained belt--suspenders fallback edge cases render option overridden user code.
code: knitr::opts_chunk$set(render = statdown_knit_render)

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Set unconditionally knitr::knit() runs: knitr::opts_knit$set(rmarkdown.pandoc.= "markdown") — ensures inline R expressions formatted Markdown output.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Set via set_suggested_options(): Users can override individual chunks setup chunk, since knitr evaluates per-chunk options defaults set. Design rule: options set deterministically. statdown probe whether settings already exist try merge pre-existing state.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: statdown follows fail-fast approach throughout: runtime probing. package check method resolution order, inspect knitr internals, guess dependency shapes. registers methods via standard R mechanisms trusts dispatch. Missing context error. knit_print.htmlwidget called outside statdown_render() session (.e. without active DependencyManager), get_dm() raises immediate error. Missing packages error. validate_environment() checks required packages stops actionable install command missing. Invalid input error. Files without .Rmd .Rmarkdown extensions rejected immediately.

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Returns: path rendered .md file (invisibly).

title: statdown DESIGN
text: Exported utility checks whether required packages (knitr, depkit, htmltools) installed. Called automatically statdown_render() can also called directly.

what: You have a site. You want to add R.
title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: already blog built Hugo, Jekyll, Eleventy, another static site generator. theme like, build pipeline works, shortcodes ’ve written. Now want include R output — plot, table, maybe interactive widget — post. standard advice reach blogdown Quarto. work, change relationship site generator.

title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: blogdown routes .Rmd Pandoc Hugo sees . Quarto . result two independent Markdown--HTML engines series: Pandoc SSG Markdown--HTML converters, different converters different rules. opinions CommonMark extensions, raw HTML handling, template features. creates practical problem: write two parsers .
code: .Rmd / .qmd   → knitr (evaluates R)     → Pandoc (converts Markdown to HTML)       → Hugo / Jekyll (converts Markdown to HTML again)         → final site

title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: Hugo shortcodes like {{</* figure src="plot.svg" */>}} must survive Pandoc processing untouched. means guarding — wrapping raw HTML blocks, using special escape syntax, writing separate shortcode versions Pandoc-processed content plain Markdown content. isn’t one-time setup cost. Every shortcode write adopt account whether content passed Pandoc first. Authors working Hugo side must think Pandoc escaping, unnatural someone working Hugo’s template language.

what: Two Markdown dialects in one site
title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: blogdown Quarto, .Rmd / .qmd files go Pandoc SSG. Plain .md files go SSG . two paths produce subtly different HTML similar-looking Markdown. CSS JS Pandoc’s template system can conflict SSG theme. result context-switching: Pandoc-flavoured Markdown R files, SSG-flavoured Markdown everything else. Similar enough look interchangeable, different enough break.

what: statdown: drop Pandoc, keep your SSG
title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: statdown takes opposite approach Quarto. Instead pulling website R’s toolchain, pushes R output SSG’s world: knitr HTML engine. evaluates R code chunks inserts results — text, tables, plot references, widget HTML — Markdown source. parse transform surrounding Markdown. output plain CommonMark optional raw HTML blocks. One system converts Markdown HTML: static site generator. write one parser, one set rules, one mental model.
code: .Rmd   → knitr (evaluates R, splices results into Markdown)     → CommonMark .md (with raw HTML blocks for widgets)       → Hugo / Jekyll / any SSG (the only HTML engine)         → final site

previous_headings: What this looks like in practice
title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: statdown outputs CommonMark, SSG features pass knitr unchanged. Hugo shortcodes, Jekyll includes, Eleventy shortcodes just text Markdown source — escaping, guarding, special cases. statdown_render(), .md output contains plot reference shortcode verbatim. Hugo processes . Pandoc mangle shortcode syntax.
code: --- title: "My Post" ---  Here is a plot generated by R:  ```{r} plot(cars) ```  And here is a Hugo shortcode, used normally:  {{</* youtube dQw4w9WgXcQ */>}}

title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: knitr encounters htmlwidget (e.g. DT datatable leaflet map), statdown’s custom knit_print method: Extracts widget’s CSS/JS dependencies Registers depkit, deduplicates copies assets Emits widget’s HTML inline knitr finishes, statdown_render() prepends <link> tags appends <script> tags .md file. raw HTML blocks CommonMark — every compliant parser passes final page unchanged. default, JS assets served CDN (jsDelivr) SRI integrity hashes automatic local fallback. reduces hosting burden without sacrificing reliability.

text: one engine, Pandoc Markdown vs. Hugo Markdown distinction. entire site uses Markdown dialect: whatever SSG implements. R posts plain posts processed identically.

what: When Quarto is the better choice
title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: Quarto solves two-engine problem differently: eliminates SSG. Quarto becomes site builder, one engine — ’s Pandoc, Hugo. valid approach R center site rather occasional guest. Quarto also provides capabilities statdown : Citations crossrefs — Pandoc’s citation processor cross-reference system mature powerful. Multi-format output — one source document can produce HTML, PDF, slides, Word. statdown produces CommonMark .md. Quarto extensions — growing ecosystem filters, formats, shortcodes operate Pandoc layer. Integrated publishing — quarto publish handles deployment GitHub Pages, Netlify, hosts. workflow involves academic writing, multi-format delivery, heavy use Pandoc features, Quarto better tool.

title: Why One HTML Engine Matters
text: statdown bloggers happen use R — people want keep static site generator add R content without second HTML engine pipeline. outputting CommonMark letting SSG handle HTML conversion, shortcodes work without workarounds, ’s one Markdown dialect across whole site, R guest build process rather host.

title: statdown
text: Use R static site — without replacing static site generator. statdown renders .Rmd files CommonMark .md Hugo, Jekyll, Eleventy, SSG can consume directly. Pandoc, second HTML engine, shortcode workarounds. SSG stays charge; R just provides computed content.

title: statdown
text: Tools like blogdown Quarto solve R + website pulling website R’s toolchain. blogdown routes output Pandoc Hugo sees , means two HTML engines process every page. Shortcodes must guarded Pandoc. Templates behave differently depending whether content came .Rmd .md. Quarto goes replaces SSG entirely. good tools, work best R center site. ’re blogger happens use R — someone already Hugo theme, build pipeline, opinions site works — ask give control don’t want give .

title: statdown
text: statdown goes direction: just enough R scaffolding produce Markdown SSG already understands. One engine. knitr evaluates R code produces CommonMark. SSG processes Markdown templates shortcodes. Pandoc middle. htmlwidgets work. Interactive widgets (DT, leaflet, plotly) rendered inline HTML CSS/JS dependencies managed depkit — deduplicated, copied asset directory, served via CDN SRI integrity default. SSG stays charge. Front matter, shortcodes, templates, build steps SSG’s business. statdown doesn’t touch .
code: .Rmd → knitr → .md → your SSG → site

title: statdown
code: flowchart LR      subgraph K[statdown]     A1(.Rmd) --> A2(knitr)     A2 --> A3(.md)     A3 --> A4(SSG)     A4 --> A5(Site)     end      subgraph B[blogdown]     B1(.Rmd) --> B2(knitr)     B2 --> B3(Pandoc)     B3 --> B4(Hugo)     B4 --> B5(Site)     end      subgraph Q[Quarto]     Q1(.qmd) --> Q2(knitr)     Q2 --> Q3(Pandoc)     Q3 --> Q4(Site)     end

title: statdown
text: output .md contains: - CSS <link> tags top (htmlwidgets used) - Markdown content inline HTML widgets - JS <script> tags bottom (CDN + SRI + local fallback) Drop SSG’s content directory build usual.
code: library(statdown)  # Render an .Rmd to .md statdown_render("posts/my-analysis.Rmd")  # Custom asset directory and URL prefix statdown_render("posts/my-analysis.Rmd",   output_root = "static/libs",   url_root = "/libs")  # Serve all widget assets locally (no CDN) statdown_render("posts/my-analysis.Rmd", cdn = FALSE)

title: statdown
text: ? Bloggers use static site generator sometimes need R — R users need website. ’re happy Quarto blogdown, keep using . blogdown .Rmarkdown files? blogdown’s .Rmarkdown path avoids Pandoc, still inherit blogdown’s opinions directory structure, Hugo integration, build workflow. statdown single function opinions site. Quarto? Quarto excellent R-centric sites, books, documents. want site generator understand R natively, use Quarto. want existing SSG stay charge, use statdown. need handle front matter? statdown passes front matter unchanged. Use whatever SSG expects.

what: Render an R Markdown file to CommonMark — statdown_render
title: Render an R Markdown file to CommonMark — statdown_render
text: Wraps knitr::knit() render .Rmd .Rmarkdown files CommonMark .md. htmlwidgets present, HTML inlined CSS/JS dependency tags injected via depkit.

title: Render an R Markdown file to CommonMark — statdown_render
code: statdown_render(   input,   output_root = NULL,   url_root = NULL,   cdn = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Render an R Markdown file to CommonMark — statdown_render
text: input character string specifying input file (must .Rmd .Rmarkdown extension). output_root Character. Filesystem directory depkit copies widget assets. Defaults libs/ directory next input file. url_root Character. URL prefix used emitted <link> / <script> tags. Defaults "libs". cdn Logical. TRUE (default), depkit emits CDN URLs SRI integrity JS assets, falling back local copies CDN unreachable. Set FALSE serve assets locally. quiet logical value indicating whether suppress knitr output messages (default: FALSE).

title: Render an R Markdown file to CommonMark — statdown_render
text: path rendered .md file (invisibly).

title: Render an R Markdown file to CommonMark — statdown_render
text: output .md compatible static site generator accepts CommonMark (Hugo, Jekyll, Eleventy, etc.). output filename derived replacing input extension .md.
